reviews.title: Acceptable

reviews.negatives: The AC did not work well AT ALL, so even in May we were super hot all night and could not sleep. We opened the window, but there was no breeze and with the windows open the noise of the station kept us awake all night. There was no solution! Also, the entire hotel is rather old and tired and could definitely use a renovation. The breakfast buffet was also not very tasty.The website mentions a gym, but staff told us that it hasn't been available since COVID.

reviews.title: This hotel is wretched!
reviews.positives: Beds were comfortable.
reviews.negatives: This place is a DUMP!  We were shocked when we got into our room.  Floors are absolutely disgusting, stains all over.  The bathroom was moldy, caulk around the sink, shower walls, even the door had black surface mold growing.  Pretty much every crevice in the bathroom had mold.  There was no caulk around the tub and about a 6 MM gap, I don't want to imagine what was underneath.  The smell was also off-putting, main room and bathroom both stank.   I immediately went to the lobby and asked if this was normal, they gave us another room that really wasn't much better. Maybe a few less stains on the carpet and less smell in the bathroom of pee.  The walls throughout the hotel were stained and wallpaper ripped.  Base molding was filthy wherever you looked.  The only positive was it was only one night.

reviews.room_type: Triple room, No view, Non-smoking (Triple Room [Non-Smoking] ☆ Free High-Speed Wi-Fi)

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: The staff were professional, always available, and friendly/helpful when needed. The location is great, so close to the Narita JR and Keisei stations with many restaurants and convenience stores nearby.
reviews.negatives: The temperature in the guest rooms are controllable. We were informed the 'off' 'L' 'M' and 'H' buttons were all to control how much air enters the room, but we had no control over the temperature itself. In the winter all rooms get warm air and so the room was stuffy and too warm for us to sleep well. We tried opening the window but it doesn't stay open due to the weight of the curtains AND the train station is directly below the window so we were woken by platform announcements, whistles, and chimes all night/early morning. We didn't get good sleep at all for all (3) nights we spent there.

reviews.title: Very Disappointing Stay
reviews.text:
The room was quite old, dirty, and had an unpleasant smell. As soon as I opened the door, I noticed a strong cigarette odor, even though I had booked a non-smoking room.
The bathroom facilities were very outdated and difficult to use. The toilet did not flush properly and there was a strong musty smell. I also found hair on the bathroom floor, which was very unhygienic.
The hand soap bottle was broken and could not be pressed, and the soap inside was less than half full. Overall, the bathroom looked poorly maintained and gave a very bad impression.
The bed was extremely old, the mattress was worn out and uncomfortable, and the pillow was too small to sleep on properly.
Overall, this stay was very disappointing and did not meet basic expectations for cleanliness and comfort.

reviews.title: Strong sale smoke smell throughout hotel
reviews.text: Very strong smell of stale smoke in all areas of hotel. No air con in the room and window locked. Hotel very worn with stained carpets and worn fixtures and fittings. I’d have left and booked somewhere else but I was exhausted after a long flight.

reviews.title: Bad smell
reviews.text: Felt like entire place was damp.

reviews.title: Creepy and deserted.
reviews.positives: The rooms were big
reviews.negatives: The hotel is very outdated. There are not many guests so there is this creepy haunting feeling throughout the hotel especially the hallways and elevators. At night time, the lights are dim and they play the creepy violin music around the elevator. The elevator is located next to the conference meeting hall. At night, the lights at the Conference Hall are off so you cannot see more than 6ft into the darkness. That coupled with the  creepy violin music is like a scene right out of a horror movie.

reviews.title: Room wasn’t clean and smelled weird
reviews.text: The buildings looked to be in a state of minor disrepair as well. Did not enjoy my stay and will never go back.

reviews.title: Good for one night only.
reviews.positives: Right next to Narita Station. Perfect for just one night. Super cool restaurant in the hotel as well.
reviews.negatives: This hotel is very.. unsettling. It feels like its a liminal space found in a horror movie. There is no A/C and the beds are incredibly firm (and this is AFTER my whole japan trip). The shower pressure was painful, so I had to let it dribble water instead.

reviews.title: Buffet breakfast is bad. Staff not helpful. Room is v v small.
reviews.text: Not recommended. And v nosy during night because of the trains.

reviews.title: Very disappointed and surprised how dirty this hotel was.
reviews.positives: This hotel is located close to Keisei Narita and JR train station. Very convenient to restaurants and old Narita town is within walking distance.
reviews.negatives: This hotel is very old. We were assigned a hotle room which we had to cross over to another building. The  rooms where very old and dirty The rooms are not very clean and the bathroom looked like no one had cleaned it for a while. I brought Clorox wipes and wiped down my bathroom as best I could.  There was black mold on the ceiling, walls and the toilet.  The hallway and rooms had a musty smell. The hallways smelled of cigarettes.

reviews.title: A Little TLC Would Help
reviews.text:
Stayed for one night at this hotel. The pros - a great location just a short train or subway ride from the airport. A nice little neighborhood with plenty of restaurant options. A good sized room.
The negatives. The hotel is in need of an update. Carpets are worn and not very clean. Really the least nice hotel room I stayed in during two weeks of travel in Japan. There is a health or fitness club that they charge extra for  - so I cannot comment on it. A bit of a sad and neglected place which could offer a really nice stay if some money was invested in it. I would suggest you look at options in same location.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: The hotel too old and dirty

reviews.title: Not what we expected
reviews.text: The location is not bad, and the parking is free, but the room isn't really clean, because there is a weird smell, and there are stains on the wall. The breakfast also isn't great, as there wasn't much to choose from, and it was the same food for 3 consecutive days.

reviews.title: Dirty hotel
reviews.text: Other than the good location, the hotel is maintained very poorly. So glad I was only staying one night, and couldn’t wait to leave. My worst hotel experience in Japan.

reviews.title: Shocking Japan Standards
reviews.text: We checked in late at 2300H. I was shocked when I went into the bathroom. It smells really bad with urine stains all over the toilet bowl and floor. I had to use hot water to rinse it off. It was late and we were too tired to move to another room. It was raining and there were no air con but hot air coming from the central air con. Only selection of Low Med and High.  Window can be opened but the there were constant grinding noise coming from outside. We didn’t rest well at all. That will be my last stay at that hotel.

reviews.title: Old tired dirty rooms from 70s
reviews.text:
Close to train station and that is the only good thing about it. I booked a twin room but i didnt know only twin room is available in the old run down annex building from the 70s. Esculator at front entrance was broken. One out of three elevators were broken for a long time. Gym closed since Covid and never reopen. 

Room is big but old and run down. We changed rooms three times. First room had a mouldy smell and is next to railtrack. Very noisy and cannot sleep n relax when train go pass every 3 minutes. Second room had faulty main lights and engineer was not on site to fix it. Third room had a curry smell but i was too tired to move to a fourth room .

Staff helpfulness is a hit and miss. Female staff is generally friendly and helpful but male staff such as Mr 川原 (Kawahara?) just refused to do his job and  provide any service, such as From answering queries to taking luggage to temp storage at all. I had to literally FORCE him to issue luggage tag and take in the luggage while he pretend to be busy counting blank paper from his reception drawer while avoiding eye contact.

reviews.title: No longer worth it
reviews.text: It's become a lot more expensive since 2019, and so for more money you get a much smaller room. The room was scruffy at best, very warm with a half-working aircon that took literally hours to get the room down to a passable temperature. The aircon is built into the bedding furniture, and it was absolutely disgusting. I can't believe that a machine so disgusting is allowed to blow any air into the room without health inspection closing the whole annex wing (which is where I was). Other than this, it was an okay business hotel.

reviews.title: Worse hotel ever stay in japan
reviews.text: Location is really good if you taking early morning flight and arrive late at night. HOWEVER, this hotel is really outdated! The room we stay is not clear at all a lot of stuffs in the room has old stain on it and it was rusted. Therefore i do not recommend this hotel unless you exhausted your other choices c/z tokyo hotels are expensive during weekend

reviews.title: Air conditioning stinks. But otherwise ok
reviews.positives: Good location,  nice hotel,  staff were good
reviews.negatives: All guests complained about hot rooms.  Air con not blowing cold had everyone hot. I complained and they tried to give me another room that was exactly the same situation and smelled like cigarettes so i declined.  Got a fan from the staff.  Windows do open on 11th floor so i could get some air,  thank God. Outlets not convenient and no usb ports. Otherwise it was tolerable but i won't stay there again.

reviews.title: I left right away
reviews.text: The place does not really look like the pictures. The Place is so old and smells like moldy carpet. The beds were springy and uncomfortable with only 1 tiny pillow. I didn’t bother staying. I just left and stayed in another hotel to avoid the headache. The only good thing about this place is the location. Its very near the train station and walking distance to places to see. I wish they would revamp and renovate the whole place. It’s such a shame.

reviews.title: Easy access but old building
reviews.positives:
Easy access to stations and restaurants and drug store.
Rooms were fairly large for Japanese hotel.  I believe our room had 22meter squared.
reviews.negatives: The building is outdated like a decade ago.  Not enough electrical outlets and no electrical outlet by nightstand.  Air conditioners were so outdated that it only has high, medium, low, off.  No easily visible clock, so we had to keep looking at my wrist watch.  Hotel has 2 buildings, and to access the “newer” building, you have to enter through main building, go up to 3rd floor and cross the walkway and access another elevator to your room.  It’s good if you’re just staying the night to and straight to sleep, but I would put it lower priority on the list if you have other accommodation in mind.

reviews.title: Only thing Great is location
reviews.text: It is not up to the quality as it was named “Mercure narita hotel” . The room was old and need to update. We booked 2 nights but need to update the key card on the second day because it wont open the door.  It was quite annoying to have to take 2 elevator and walk down to the counter to update the card. There is a mini fridge but no water bottle in the room. Guest need to take it by themselves, but nobody were giving this info.  We book non-smoking room and we got no- smoking  room but we can smell the cigarettes smoke in the hallway to the room. The only great thing about this hotel is the location that is right in middle of the JR narita station and Keisei Narita station.

reviews.title: There should have better choices
reviews.text: Although I am a frequent traveler to Japan, this was the first time stayed in this hotel. Compare to others, the price with the location like this is definitely the best as it is close to train station and bus terminal, if you don't mind the hotel just located right next to the railway track. But the room quality can ruin all your good impression. I am not saying the cleanliness, the room is average clean, we stayed in the extended building not the main hotel's building, the air-conditioning was awful, the controller only got 3 levels to adjust (low, mid, high), even you turned to the level high, the room still feel hot and humid, the service was good, after reporting the room status, they gave us an electric fan to cool down the room temperature. If you do not mind the room is a bit suffocating and you only stay for a night and departing from Japan the next day, this hotel could be on your list.

reviews.title: While the location is a major plus, the age of the facility may not meet everyone’s expectations.
reviews.positives: hotel’s location is fantastic! It’s conveniently situated near the stations.
reviews.negatives: I found that the facilities could use some updates. The overall appearance feels a bit dated

reviews.title: Old hotel
reviews.text:
Honestly, skip this place if you’re on vacation. The building is super old and my room didn't even have AC. The staff actually told me to just leave the window open to stay cool, but then I had to deal with the constant noise from the trains outside.

To top it off, there was a control panel in the middle of the room that literally said "Air Con," but it turned out to be a heater. Definitely not the vibe I wanted for my trip.

reviews.negatives: Uncomfortable bed, old and tired rooms. No cold air, have to open the window if you want to cool down the room. Window is super low and opens sideways in a pivotal, very dangerous if you have kids in the room. Got what we paid for.

reviews.title: Not quite what I expected
reviews.positives: The staff was very friendly and helpful. I really enjoyed our interactions. They were able to help us out when we asked for an extra pillow and blanket. The bed linens were clean and crisp.
reviews.negatives: The bathroom was not very clean. There were hairs leftover and it was difficult to use-very outdated. I think it looked more dirty because of how old the grout and caulking was. The hallways smelled of mold and the carpets seemed to have mold stains growing from the walls. Luckily we were only staying one quick night between flights, I would’ve been very disappointed if this had been where we stayed during a longer trip.

reviews.title: I am neutral about this hotel
reviews.text: Location is good, and the bed is clean. But Bathtub is dirty, and I had to stand and shower. The annex build corridor is smelly, and the laundry room is terrible.  I am neutral about it.

reviews.title: Overall, while the location and staff were highlights, the cleanliness and comfort of the rooms left much to be desired.
reviews.positives: recently stayed at Welco Hotel Narita and had a mixed experience. On the positive side, the staff were incredibly friendly and accommodating. The hotel is conveniently located near train stations and has a convenience store right in front, making it easy to grab essentials.
reviews.negatives: However, there were several drawbacks. The room was uncomfortably hot, and we requested two electric fans just to sleep comfortably. Additionally, we found the cleanliness of the rooms to be lacking. Our original room was dusty, and the bed sheets didn’t seem fresh. The staff promptly transferred us to another room, but unfortunately, the new room also had a lot dust under the bed. The carpet was stained, and both the room and hallway had a smoke smell that was quite off-putting. At night, the hallways felt a bit eerie!

reviews.title: A very convenient hotel near the old street
reviews.text: A very convenient hotel near the old street with very friendly and helpful stuff.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: The location was great and the staff were kind.
reviews.negatives: The property is outdated. Our room was way too hot, so I called the front desk and asked for a fan. A staff came over with two fans. We slept well after that. So, thanks so much for making us feel more comfortable.

reviews.title: Cleanliness
reviews.text:
It is an old hotel and  smells bad. Especially the hallway, room was smelly too, but I was able to open the window and get some fresh air.

Shower curtain in the bathroom had mold on it and  smells so bad.
I called the receptionist to change it.
Bathtub was  so old and black mold on the side of  it 

Hotel staff were very nice and friendly.

reviews.title: Booked non-smoking room but
reviews.text: the room is so smelly of old, stale smoke. that we could not sleep. couldn't wait to check out. Richmond is better in many ways

reviews.title: Non-smoking room felt like a smoking room.
reviews.positives:
We got a different room when we asked for it without much question.
It was near the station and Narita airport, which was convenient.
reviews.negatives:
We got a room in the annex building first, where the room was too warm and the atmosphere was bad, it seemed like there was little to no ventilation, there was no aircon control and it seemed as if the window couldn't open (it could though).
We asked for a different room in an attempt to get better conditions, which we got, where the room was slightly less worn, so we could see stickers there hinting at how the window had to open, so we opened the window fully and let the room ventilate for hours to get rid of the poor air, it almost seemed as if the room was once a smoking room.

reviews.title: I wouldn't recommend it
reviews.text: The room that was given to us smelled musty and the ac wasn't as cold but we didn't complain because we only had 1 night there.  The woman at the front desk was being kind of rude when I asked if we could do a late check out (1 hour). I stayed at the hotel a few times but this time service and room wasn't as I expected.

reviews.title: Not Recommend
reviews.text: Hotel location is excellent but it is old and not well-kept.  We booked a twin room and was told our room is located in the annex section.  Not sure if rooms in the main building are any better but our room in the annex was old and dirty., especially the bathroom.  The beds are too soft and the room is damp.  The wifi was not working and when I asked the reception, he kept saying just follow the instructions on the card board.   I just gave up since we're only staying for 1 night.  It was the worst hotel we stayed at during our 20 days in Japan.

reviews.title: Train track is next to our room
reviews.text: The hotel is in good location but the train track is just next to our room. We couldn’t sleep as train pass by every 5 mins.

reviews.title: Cleanliness an issue
reviews.text: Carpets were filthy. Dust and furballs around edges of bed, a hair scrungy lying abt.

reviews.title: Train noise and temperature trouble
reviews.text:
This was a great location and seemed like the price was right. We were in the annex on third floor and unfortunately the train warning sound was very loud. It made it worse that we had to open the window because the room was very hot and only a heater was available this time of year. reviews.title: The location is very good.
reviews.text:
The Keisei Narita Station and Narita Station are both very conveniently located within a 5-minute walk. There are convenience stores, drugstores, and restaurants nearby, so you won’t have any trouble even if you stay for an extended period. Amenities can be requested at the front desk, and the rooms are spacious. I think this hotel is suitable for inbound travelers. 

The building shows signs of aging. In the room I stayed in, there were areas on the bathroom floor that sank when stepped on, which was a bit concerning. There are stains on the floors and curtains, so it may not be suitable for those seeking cleanliness. There were also other dirt spots in the bathroom aside from the wear and tear, which became noticeable since I stayed for a longer duration.

The staff members are very courteous. However, there seems to be a communication issue among the staff, as sometimes my requests were not accurately conveyed. 

During cleaning, they replaced my dishes, but for some reason, new dishes were placed on the desk and console table. I wondered if this was an effort to "place new dishes where the used ones were," but it made me anxious about whether they were truly new and they would collect dust. I believe it would be more pleasant if the replaced dishes were returned to their designated storage. 

Since I would like to use this hotel again, I wanted to express my thoughts honestly.

In terms of room, the room is a bit old. I stayed at 8th floor and you cannot adjust the air-conditioning as it is centralized. 

This hotel will definitely be my option again next time if my flight is within the peak period. 

Nevertheless, I enjoyed my stay due to the location which is right behind the train station.

reviews.text: I booked 2 rooms here for the night after we experienced an overnight delay at Narita Airport. We were exhausted after a whole day of traveling. I managed to find this place on Agoda and booked it very quickly as everything within airport radius was being sold out. Upon arriving, we were greeted by a female receptionist, who was professional and kind. While waiting to be checked in, a male receptionist with glasses was exhibiting very unprofessional behavior whenever his female colleague tried to assist us with our bags and some goodies. He motioned to her not to give us said goodies and to not help us with anything like our bags. I was traveling with my family which consisted of my wife, a senior, and a 2 year old baby. He did so in a way that was very much obvious as I was not the only one to observe it, as did my wife and another female employee in a backroom. To that gentleman, I will be writing a formal complaint to the respective parties which need to know about such conduct.

The rooms themselves are comfortable enough, but mediocre at best. The hotel is a bit rundown, and has certainly seen better days. However, for the price we paid, we really couldn't expect more. reviews.title: Ok
reviews.text:
Very dated. No temperature control for air con. Toilet was running, room very dated. Bed with springs, some of which were very noticeable. 
Front desk staff very nice, housekeeping passed with no acknowledgement.  Bathroom smelled like mildew. 
Great location situated between JR and Kesei lines. 
Fast internet. Clean linens. Laundry facility. 
Good budget hotel in a great location. Shop around.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.positives: Breakfast was good.
reviews.negatives:
The room was so warm that we had to open the window. 
The beds were very hard. The pillow was hard and too small.

I stay in different building
like a maze
